expect: Identify structural gap preventing external validation loop closure. Wire at least one enforcement mechanism.
actual: Gap fully diagnosed but enforcement already partially wired. orient.py already surfaces prediction deadlines (7-day window). market-review periodic exists (cadence=10, DUE at S530). L-1542 written to fill F-FLT2 lesson gap (97 sessions overdue). Phantom L-1424 annotated. The binding constraint is NOT missing tooling — it's that PRED-0017 (first resolvable prediction) doesn't expire until 2026-03-29. Structural enforcement exists; the system is waiting for reality to produce the first data point.
diff: Expected to find missing enforcement. Found that enforcement exists but the external world has a 5-day latency (PRED-0017 resolve date). The swarm cannot speed up external reality — it can only prepare infrastructure. Infrastructure is ready: orient.py deadline display, market_predict.py resolve/score, market-review periodic. The first genuine external data point will land ~S535.
effect_size: F-FLT2 lesson gap closed after 97 sessions (S433→S530). External validation remains 0% — waiting for PRED-0017.
surprise: LOW — enforcement already exists. The real finding is that the gap is temporal (waiting for external world), not structural (missing infrastructure).
successor: S535: resolve PRED-0017 (SPY BEAR, conf=0.1, baseline 648.57). Run market_predict.py score after first resolution. This will be first non-zero external validation in swarm history.
